---
title: "ED Fig 2c - pooled-replicate neurite-actin xcorr (SD envelope)"
output: html_notebook
---

<!--
================================================================================
  WRAPPER for ED Fig 2c (pooled-replicate CCF with SD envelope).
================================================================================
  This file's role: place the canonical analysis in the EDFig02_actin
  directory so `Path_1 <- dirname(...)` resolves here, picking up the
  Fig 2e dataset's per-cell CSV inputs.

  Includes the canonical template:
      ../../templates/pooled_replicate_ccf.Rmd

  Manuscript panel:
      ED Fig 2c "Cross-correlation of temporal profiles of DIV-1 and DIV-2
                 unpolarized neurons. Same in Fig. 2e but with SD. Values
                 plotted as mean +/- SD. N = 135 neurons, 5 experiments."

      (Also feeds the secondary visualisation behind ED Fig 2e/f for the
       replicate-pooling alternative summary.)

  Original G:\ source:
      G:/Figure 2_actin wave/Fig. 2e/R_scripts/Neurite activity corr_comb.Rmd
      (_comb suffix = combined-replicate, i.e. pooled).

  Sibling that includes the same template (different data directory):
      figures/EDFig01_coordination/edfig01be_2d_culture_neurite_freq_and_xcorr_pooled.Rmd
      (ED Fig 1b; 2D-culture pooled-replicate CCF, N = 101).

  What is unique vs the fig02e canonical
      CCF algorithm is identical to `fig02e_neurite_actin_xcorr_culture.Rmd`.
      The difference is the post-CCF aggregation: groups CCF curves by
      experimental replicate and reports mean +/- SD envelope across
      replicates, rather than mean +/- SD across individual neurons.

  Refactored 2026-05-12: prior 2344-line wrapper had a body byte-identical
  to its sibling; both moved to a shared canonical template.

  Essentiality: Essential.
-->

```{r child = "../../templates/pooled_replicate_ccf.Rmd"}
```
